def: s.m. = اژدها azhdahā, q.v. s.m. A large serpent, python, boa constrictor ( = ajgar); a dragon.
adj. Very heavy or ponderous; unwieldy.
more: n. m. Old P. اَژدو P. اَژدﮩـا S. अञ a goat, गृ to swallow. lit . The goat-eater. A large serpent or snake; a dragon; boa constrictor; python. 1. Ajgar † kare na chākrī, panchhī kare na kām. Dās Malūkā yoṅ kahe, sab ke Dātā Rām . Malūka Dās. The dragon no one serves, no work the bird performs, But God, who gives to all, doth feed them, saith Malūka Dās. 2. Ajgar ke Dātā, Rām . Prov. God gives to all. [Said by a lazy fellow who spends money but wont work). adj. Very heavy; weighty; unwieldly (bhārī). Ṭolī to ajgar hai, masaktī bhī nahīṅ . The stone is very heavy, I can't move it in least.
s. m. A large serpent, the Boa constrictor.
ex: اجگر ایک مامولی سانپ سے زیادہ خطرناک ہوتا ہے۔
ajgar ek māmūlī sā̃p se zyāda xatarnāk hotā hai.
The python happens to be more dangerous than a run-of-the-mill snake.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit अजगर (ajagara).

def: adj. Wonderful, marvellous, strange, surprising
more: n. f. S. अति + उद्गत, Pālī. achchuggato raised. 1. A phenomenon; a wonder; impossibility. Ek ajgut maiṅ aisā dekhā, ek gadhā do sīṅgh, * Chīṅṭī ke gal paghā dekhā, kheṅcheṅ Arjun, Bhīm . Kabīr. A wondrous thing I saw, a donkey with two horns, A rope around its neck, an ant dragged both Arjun and Bhīm. [This is a satire on the world, where fools are often elevated to power, and small things are accomplished by elaborate contrivances]. 2. Precocity; intelligence beyond one's years. Yeh laṛkinyā ajgut karela, chhawe baras ke ba, sau tak le gin jālis! Bhoj. This little girl does wonders. She is only six years old, and she can count up to a hundred. 3. Flippancy; sauciness; impudence. 4. Wrong; evil; harm; mischief (burāī). Jān bījh ajgut kare, tā se kahā basāë Sabhā Bilās. If one will do wrong knowingly, what can you do with him. 5. Violence; oppression; tyranny; cruelty. Tūṅ baṛ ajgut kaila…
subs: wonder, marvel, prodigy; evil, harm, wrong; violence, outrage, oppression, tyranny ( = غضب g̠aẓab; in these senses ajgut would appear to be identical with ajugat or ajukt = S. ayukta).

def: adj. More, or most, ignorant, very ignorant: — s.m. A blockhead.
more: adj. compar. (of جاهل) More, most or very ignorant.

def: adj. Lit. 'Not shaking or swinging'; of steady gait or pace (applied to an elephant).

ety: S. अ interj.+जी, contr. fr. जीव
def: Interj. (used in addressing, or to call attention, or to express surprise, or contempt), ho there! I say; Sir! O sir! Good sir! Madam! My lady; good woman or good man (forms used by a husband and wife respectively in addressing each other, it being considered disrespectful for them to call each other by name).
more: n. f. 1. A grandmother, E. Nātin sikhāwe ājī ko, ke bārā ḍeoṛhe āṭh . Prov. The granddaughter teaches her grandmother "twelve times one and a half are eight!" (Teach your grandmother to suck eggs). 2. A mother-in-law (sās) . W. Hin. Wom.

An interjection to call or bespeak attention. (In Bengal, it is used to an inferiour or in cases of great intimacy only; but, in the western provinces, it is often addressed to a superiour as a term of respect). Holla! Good sir! Hear me, sir!

def: s.f. (from ājā), A grandmother.
more: intj. S. अहो, Pr. ajja expressive of calling, surprise, contempt. 1. A form of address, as, O; I say; sir; friend; look here. Ajī! jāne bhī do, kis ke hage meṅ mūṅh ḍālte ho? My good sir, let it pass. Are you going to put your mouth in any one's filth? 2. My dear madam; my dear lady; my dear sir. 3. The form of address used by a wife in addressing her husband whose name it is considered disrespectful to take, and vice versâ .

def: adj. Not decomposed; unimpaired; undigested; — indigestible; unwholesome, injurious, pernicious; disagreeable, distasteful, sickening, nauseating, wearying, wearisome, tedious, sick or weary (of); persistent, unremitting; inveterate, chronic (as a disease); excessive (so as to produce nausea, disgust, &c.); hardy, strong; — s.m. Surfeit, indigestion; flatulence; nausea, &c.
subs: To be sickening, nauseating, wearying, &c.; to be surfeited; to be glutted (lit. & fig.); to be produced in abundance, to abound.

def: Gaṛh. adj. S. अजीर्ण, अ not, जृ to be digested. 1. Disagreeing with; indigestible; unwholesome. Jāo, merā khānā ajīran mat karo . 2. Hurtful; pernicious; destructive; fatal. Itnī aql bhī ajīran hoti hai . Prov. You are too sharp, you may cut yourself. 3. Distasteful; tired of; disinclined for; sick of. Roz kī dāl to ajīran ho gaī . 4. Unpleasant; displeasing; disagreeable; vexatious. Itnā haṅsnā bhī aj/?/ran hai . 5. Monotonous; flat; prosy. 6. Persistent; unremitting; incurable. Yeh bukhār to ajiran ho gayā . 7. Tedious; wearisome; tiresome ( pahāṛ ). Āj kā din ajīran ho gayā . 8. Too much (bahut) ; excessive; inordinate; spoilt through excess. Dāl meṅ namak ajīran hai . 9. Excessively hard; strong; difficult. A jīran ko ajīran hī ṭhele. Nahīṅ to sir chauhaṭṭe khele . E. Prov. Let the strong contend with the strong. or your head shall be a foot-ball for the way-farer. 10. Irksome; intolerable; insupportable. Par chand roz ko ham ho jāëṅge ajīran, Yūṅ hī rahā karegā ham se agar takalluf . Inshā. A few days more, and I intol'rable must be to you, If all this labored court'sy you each day renew. 11. Burdensome; heavy; oppressive. Itnā sīnā ajīran hai. ajīran ho'nā , v. n. 1. To be burdensome, tiresome, etc. 2. To grow in abundance; to become a glut (in the market).
s. m. Indigestion; surfeit, flatulence.

ety: act. part. of عجز 'to lack strength, to be powerless,' &c.
def: adj. Lacking strength or power, or ability, powerless, impotent, unable (to do), unequal (to); weak, feeble, helpless; brought low, overcome; lowly, humble; exhausted; dejected; in despair, hopeless; baffled, frustrated
more: adj. عجز was unable. 1. Incapable; unable; crippled; overcome ( be-bas ). 2. Baffled; baulked, frustrated. ājiz ānā yā h. , v. n. To be rendered helpless, Āte haiṅ milte milte jo ājiz parī-rukhāṅ, Dete haiṅ milne-vāloṅ ko ghabrā-ke gāliyāṅ, Tis par bhī lipṭe jāte haiṅ jūṅ guṛ pe makkhīyāṅ, Dāman ke ṭukṛe uṛte haiṅ, phaṭtī haiṅ dhajjiyāṅ, Kyā kyā maze haiṅ Id ke āj Id-gāh meṅ! Nazir. Now beauties fair in helpless close embrace are seen, Though met with showers of abuse these greetings keen Yet still they stick on close as flies on sweets serene, Their garments torn to shreds are scattered all atween Such rapturous joys today illume this Id-gah-scene! ājiz k. , v. a. 1. To render helpless ( zer k. , 2). Zarā se bachche ne ājiz kar dīyā .
subs: or ʻājiz rahnā, or ʻājiz honā (-se), To be helpless (against); to be unequal (to), to be unable to do, to fail or fall short (of, or in respect of); to despair (of), &c.
v.t. To render (one) powerless or helpless; to bring low, to humble, &c.

def: (for A. عاجزة, fem. of ʻājiz), adj. f. = ʻājiz, q.v. adj. Lacking strength or power, or ability, powerless, impotent, unable (to do), unequal (to); weak, feeble, helpless; brought low, overcome; lowly, humble; exhausted; dejected; in despair, hopeless; baffled, frustrated

def: s.m. Curled, flowered, gigantic swallow-wort, Calotropis gigantea (syn. madār).
more: n. m. S. अर्क. Curled, flowered gigantic swallow-wort, celebrated among native practitioners for its many medicinal qualities; Calotropis gigantea; Saccharum officinarum . Wat. "Rādhā," "Rādhā," raṭat haiṅ āk ḍhāk, aur kair, Tulsī, yā Brij bhūm meṅ kahā Rām sūṅ bair? Tulsī Dās, "Rādhā," "Rādhā," āk ḍhāk, & kair trees call on so: O Tulsī! can then Brij land be of Rām the foe? āk kī buṛhyā , The inner, light, soft substance of the fruit of the ākh tree. Bajuz mūe sufaid us meṅ nahīṅ kuchh; Nahīṅ jūṅ ākh kī buṛhyā kahīṅ kuchh . Saudā. Besides white hair naught's left in him at all, No substance save what's in a light pith ball.
1. (Sans. अर्क ) s. m. Curled flowered gigantick swallow-wort. (Asclepias gigantea). 2. (Sans. इक्षु ) s. m. A sprout of sugar-cane.

def: s.m. A sprout of sugar-cane (cf. ankur).

n. m. Master; owner; an employer; lord.

adj. H. ek one, P. khur , H. khā , S. खाद् to eat. lit . One who eats by himself. 1. Selfish; self-seeking; greedy. 2. Akal khurā kisī ko dekh nahīṅ saktā . 2. Akal khurā jag se burā . Prov. For bad men though the world you scan, You'll ne'er find one like the envious man.

def: adj. Exempt from duty, duty-free, exempt from taxes; — hand-less.
more: n. f. H. kaṛ'ā hard; S. कडु to be hard. 1. Inflexibility; obduracy; intractableness. 2. Hardness; rigidity; rigidness; stiffness. 3. Resolvedness; fixedness; firmness. 4. Self-will; wilfulness; obstinacy; stubbornness; doggedness. 5. Constipation; costiveness. ( aṛ ). 6. Crookedness; obliquity ( ṭeṛh ). 7. Pride; arrogance; vanity; conceit. 8. Affectation; foppery; dandyism; showing off; airs; strut. 1. Na būjho, sir par is ke chīrā baldār, Azīzoṅ, yū jawānoṅ kī akaṛ hai . Walī. Deem not the foppish turband-twists the turband's are, Not so, my friends, the foppish twists of youth they are. 2. Ghaṛī bhar meṅ sab akaṛ nikāl dūṅgā . 9. Parade; pomp; show ( dikhāwā ); showiness; ostentation. Baṛī akaṛ takaṛ se sawārī nikaltī hai. akaṛ-bāz, akṛū, akṛait, aiṅṭhū , n. m. An affected person; fop; swaggerer. akaṛ , adj. Puffed up; vain; conceited; proud.
( अ and कर Tax, hand) adj. Duty free, exempt from duty, hand-less.

adj. Unprofitable, fruitless, yielding no return, serving no purpose, ineffectual, vain; bootless, useless, unserviceable
more: adj. S. अ not, किम् what, अर्थ profit. 1. Unprofitable; fruitless; yielding no return ( nir-phal ). 2. Ineffectual; vain; abortive. Kījau kahā kāj, ālī, joban akārath jāt, Pȳā bin mo kūṅ Horī jahar kī kaṭorī hai . Song. Sister, what's left to me! passes away my youthful bloom, My husband far from me—Holi's a poisoned chalice gloom. 3. Out of use; useless ( birthā ); unserviceable; condemned. Bhaīyā jī, āj tak ham sāth rāj dono mil kīnā, Tum jāo pardes, akārath hamrā jīnā . Play. Rūp Basant. akārath jā'nā,—ho'nā , v. n. 1. To be sacrificed, destroyed, lost. Chor kā māl sab koī khāë, Chor kī jān akārath jāë . Prov. The plunder of the thief all do enjoy who may; But his alone the life the forfeit's bound to pay. 2. To go off; to be scattered to the winds. Aisā nahīṅ kheleṅ khel kabhī, jā se sab janam akārath ho. Sab rāj pāṭ /?/se dūr paṛeṅ, dunyā meṅ baṛī hiqārat ho . Rūp Basant. 3. To be …

ety: S. अङ्क, — rt. अञ्च्, — +करणीयं; — cf. also वक्र
def: v.n. To be or become crooked, twisted, contorted, distorted, contracted, &c., to be bent double with cramp, spasms, draw up the limbs in pain, to writhe; to become stiff, rigid, hard, &c.; to be frozen, paralyzed, &c.; — to be affected, pompous, vain, or conceited, to strut, swagger, move or go proudly, to bridle; to assume an arrogant or insolent air; to look defiantly (at), be ready to pick a quarrel (with, -se); to find fault (with), be displeased (at, -meṅ).
more: v. n. 1. To writhe, to ache, to be cramped, to become stiff or rigid, to stiffen. 2. To strut affectedly, to give oneʼs self airs.

def: v. n. 1. To become hard, stiff, rigid; to harden; to be frozen (the person). 1. Sardī ke māre akaṛ gayā . 2. Murdā akaṛ gayā . 2. To be wrung, distorted, contracted or drawn together, cramped; to suffer from cramp, spasms, twitching, or convulsions. Jāṛā khūb paṛ rahā hai, tawaṅgar gurūr se, muflis sardī se akaṛ * rahā haī . Gālib. 3. To be affected; mince; perk oneself up. 4. To be vain of, or swelled with pride; give oneself airs. 5. To swagger; strut; show off. 1. Rizāle ne chār ḍaṅṛ pele, aur akṛā . Let a low man have four turns (at Athletics) and he begins to strut. 2. Akaṛ ke panjoṅ ke bal jo zamīn par chalte, Jo sīdhī bāt kare un se, us ko woh dalte . Mohsin. With what an air they walked this earth on tip-toe gingerly, A civil word you spoke, they pulverized you utterly. 3. Lambī ḍāṛhī farishte pakṛeṅge, Ḍāṛhī-munḍe wahāṅ bhī akṛeṅge . The angels shall the long beards pull about, The beardless even there will strut about. ☞ According to the Sharٴa or Precepts of Mohammad, all Mahomedans should wear beards, not exceeding four inches ( ek muṭṭhī do ungal ) long. But the ultra-pious affect long beards, and the waze-dār (dandies) sport very short beards or shave them altogether. 6. To put on big looks; swell; look big. Akaṛtā hai kyā dekh dekh āīnā? Hasiṅ gar-che hai tū, par itnā ghamaṅḍ! Inshā. How you do swell as in your glass your face you see? Grant, you are beautiful but then, such pride, dear me! 7. To put on an insulting manner; to be insolent. 8. To defy openly; beard; challenge ( ṭoknā ). Phr. To bid defiance; to show fight. 9. To wrangle ( jhagaṛnā ); quarrel; fight. Apnī parchhāīṅ se bhī akaṛtā hai . He'd fight with his shadow. 10. To be displeased, piqued, huffed, vexed, hurt. Hamāre kāj meṅ akaṛ-ke baiṭh rahe . Hin.

more: adv. Behind ( pīchhe , 1—3). aqab , n. m. The rear ( pīchhā ).

ety: for A. عاقبة, quasi-inf. n. of عقب 'to succeed, follow,' &c.
def: s.f. End, termination, conclusion; futurity, the future; the future state or life; — adv. In the end, finally, ultimately, at last, after all
more: n. f. عقب came after. Futurity ( ākhirat ). āqbat bigāṛnā , v. a. To risk heaven. āqibat-andesh , adj. lit . looking to the end. 1. Far-seeing; far-sighted. 2. Provident; discreet. āqibat-andeshī , n. f. Farsightedness. āqibat-andeshī k. , v. a. To look to the future; provide against. āqbat ke borye sameṭnā , v. n. To drag one's days; lead a life of misery. 1. Ziyādah jī kar kyā āqabat ke boriye sameṭoge? Prov. Saying. Do you desire long life that you may gather up all the goods of this world on the last day? 2. Bī, āqbat ke kaun sameṭegā boriye? Jo āj āë, kal gaë, is jā yunhī rahe! Wom. Jān Sāhab. Who on the last day, sister, will what's left collect? Who comes today tomorrow goes, all here just wrecked. ☞ The legend is that on the last day an old woman will be the only survivor when all are dead, and she will gather up all the goods of this world and then find to her mortification that…

(from عقب Heb. ) s. f. 1. The end, conclusion, futurity, future state. 2 Success. 3. adv. Finally, at the end, at last, after all. عاقبت انديش āﻌḳibat-andesh, Provident, prescient, considerate, discreet, looking into futurity. عاقبت اند يشي āﻌḳibat-andesh-ī, s. f. Foresight, regard to the issue, end, consequence or future.
subs: The termination of the business, the long and short of the matter; — adv. Finally, &c. ( = ʻāqibat)
adj. Looking to the end; looking into futurity; far-seeing, far-sighted; provident, prescient, discreet, prudent, considerate
s.f. Regard to the end or issue or consequence, a looking to the future; far-sightedness; foresight, prudence
(-kī), To look to the future, to make provision (for), to provide (against); to exercise prudence
(apnī), To mar or to jeopardise (one's) future state of bliss
s.m. Provision for the next world; treasure in heaven; good works; bearing trouble or affliction with patience and resignation; — an infant gone to heaven.

ety: for A. عقيدة, v.n. fr. عقد 'to tie,' &c.; see ʻaqd
def: adj. Having firm belief or faith, being firmly persuaded; faithful, believing
more: n. m. عقد being very firm. 1. Religious belief. 2. Profession of faith. aqīdah-i-mazhab . Religious vows. aqīdat-mand, khush-aqīdah , Faithful; believing. bad-aqīdah , adj. Unbelieving; sceptic.
s. m. (from عقد) Faith, belief, a fundamental article of religion, tenet.

ety: for A. عقيدة, v.n. fr. عقد 'to tie,' &c.; see ʻaqd
def: adj. Having firm belief or faith, being firmly persuaded; faithful, believing
more: s. f. (from عقد) Faith, belief, a fundamental article of religion, tenet.

ety: for A. عقيقة, s.f. fr. ʻaqīq, 'cleft,' &c.
def: s.m. The hair on the head of a new-born infant (so called because it is cut or shaved off on the sixth day); — the ceremony of shaving the head of an infant on the sixth day after childbirth, and a sacrifice made and a feast given on that day.
more: n. m. The Mah. ceremony of shaving the head of a new born infant on the sixth day after child birth, and a sacrifice and feast made on that day.
s. m. 1. The hair upon new born infants. 2. The sixth day after childbirth, or a feast given on that day.
s. m. The sixth day after childbirth, or a sacrifice made and feast given on that day.

ety: act. part. of عقل 'to be or become intelligent,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Possessing intelligence or understanding, intelligent, wise, sensible; — a wise man, a sage.
more: adj. عقل was intelligent. pl. عقلا Sensible ( budhmān ). āqilānah ٴilāj . Skilful treatment.
(fem. عاقله āﻌḳila, part. act. of عقل) adj. Wise, sensible, intelligent. (Plur. عقلا or Pers. plur. عاقلان).

def: (for A. عاقلة, fem. of ʻāqil), adj. f. = ʻāqil, q.v. adj. & s.m. Possessing intelligence or understanding, intelligent, wise, sensible; — a wise man, a sage.

ety: ʻāqil, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Wise, intelligent; skilful (e.g. ʻāqilāna ʻilāj); — wisely, prudently, &c.

ety: ʻāqil, q.v.+ī = Zend aff i = S. इ
def: s.f. Intelligence, &c. ( = ʻaql).
adj. & adv. Only, sole, single; alone, singly.

def: s.f. Sister (a respectful compellation for an elder sister, or any elderly female: cf. ākā).

wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀓𑁆𑀓 (ekka), from Sanskrit एक (eka).

def: s.m. An enormous eater, a glutton.
ex: دیس میں بہت بھیانک اکال پڑا ہے۔
des mẽ bahut bhayānak akāl paṛā hai.
There is a terrible famine happening in the nation.
wety: From Sanskrit अकाल (akāla).

def: s.f. The Indian shot-plant, Canna indica (the name is peculiar to Southern India; in Bengal the plant is called sarva-jayā. The seeds are hard, black and shining, resembling shot, for which they are sometimes used).
more: s. (dakh.) Canna indica, or the seeds of the shot plant ( Qanoon-e-islam, gloss. p. xcix).

def: s.m. Swallow-wort ( = آك āk,q.v.). s.m. Curled, flowered, gigantic swallow-wort, Calotropis gigantea (syn. madār).
